requires-package.description: npm package(s) that must be in dependencies for this rule to be applicable. If absent in consumer's package.json and auto-skip-if-missing is true, rule is SKIPPED.

policy.description: Free-form Markdown body for the rule

negative-test.description: Liveness data for ESLint-type rules: prove the guard catches its own bad corpus.

input.description: Code snippets that must each trigger the expected violation (bypass variants).

expect-violation.description: ESLint messageId or ruleId expected from each input.

eslintRuleConfig.description: Optional ESLint rule config value, replacing bare 'error' for option-bearing rules (e.g. no-restricted-imports).

liveness-mode.description: v1.5 override for the cmd/script guard-liveness mode. Mode is DERIVED from check.type by default (command→run-and-assert, script→resolve-and-run); set this only for exceptions. 'workflow-exists' = assert the named CI workflow exists + references its jobs; 'config-presence' = assert the rule's required config exists; 'exempt' = no runnable form, excluded from the gate + the fixture-required flip.

fixture.description: Liveness data for command/script rules: a violating filesystem/env state the rule's check.command/check.script must exit-non-zero on. Required ONLY for the modes that actually execute it — run-and-assert (check.type=command, or liveness-mode='run') and resolve-and-run (check.type=script). NOT required for workflow-exists / config-presence / exempt (those modes never run a fixture).

setup-script.description: Shell commands that create the violating state. Must not be a trivial force-fail (e.g. `exit 1`).

cleanup-script.description: Shell commands that restore the environment after the liveness probe.

cwd.description: Working directory for the setup and cleanup scripts.

pressure-scenario.description: RED→GREEN forcing-function data for the manual rule prober (v3). Presence is optional; v3 flips to required for manual rules after migration. A pressure-scenario is a forcing function: it must name the pressure (`pressure`) that tempts the shortcut over the rule, not merely show a violating example.

baseline-prompt.description: The AI prompt that, WITHOUT the rule enforced, produces the failing behaviour.

observable-failure.description: What the AI produces when the rule is NOT enforced (RED state).

observable-compliance.description: What the AI produces when the rule IS enforced (GREEN state). Must differ from observable-failure.

pressure.description: The forcing pressure(s) the baseline-prompt applies — what tempts the shortcut over the rule. At least one required (T-V3-A: a pressure-scenario is a forcing function, not just a violating example).

allOf.description: v1.5: fixture is REQUIRED only for the modes that execute it — run-and-assert (command, or liveness-mode='run') and resolve-and-run (script). NOT required for liveness-mode='workflow-exists' / 'config-presence' / 'exempt' (those modes never run a fixture, so a required setup-script would be inert dead data).
